item.title: Integrative genomic and metabolomic analyses identify novel associations between mQTLs and common diseases in Han Chinese.
item.description: Journal of biomedical research - While prior metabolomic genome-wide association studies (mGWASs) have identified numerous metabolite quantitative trait loci (mQTLs) in European populations, their characterization in East Asians-p...

item.title: Protein Homeostasis Screen Identifies CK2 Inhibition as a Driver of CARD9 Depletion.
item.description: ACS chemical biology - CARD9 is a genetically validated target for autoimmune disease, with human expression quantitative trait loci (eQTLs) linking elevated CARD9 expression to increased disease risk and lower CARD9 exp...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and psoriasis as causal drivers of psychiatric disorders: A two-stage Mendelian randomization study grounded in the gut-brain-skin axis.
item.description: Journal of affective disorders - Psoriasis (PsO), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and psychiatric disorders(PDs)often coexist clinically. We hypothesize the gut-brain-skin axis mediates their cross-system interactions, though ca...

item.title: A Novel PTPN2 Isoform Differentially Regulates Immune Response.
item.description: European journal of immunology - Genome-wide association studies implicate the PTPN2 gene locus (18p11.21) in risk for several autoimmune diseases, including inflammatory bowel disease. Through genetic fine mapping, we identified ...

item.title: Mendelian randomization analyses explore the relationship between inflammatory bowel disease and genitourinary diseases.
item.description: Medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), such as Crohn disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis, plays a significant role in the development of various conditions, including genitourinary diseases (GDs). Never...
